steps.title: Identify the core claim about quantities
steps.content: The astronomers specifically state that planets contain “equal or smaller quantities” of the materials found in their host stars.

steps.title: Find evidence that directly contradicts this relationship
steps.content:
Choice C — Evidence emerges that the amount of iron in some rocky planets is considerably higher than the amount in their host star. directly contradicts the claim by showing a rocky planet contains considerably more iron than its host star.

The other choices either reinforce the shared-materials foundation or question methodology for non-rocky planets, neither addressing the core quantity relationship.

That matches Choice C — Evidence emerges that the amount of iron in some rocky planets is considerably higher than the amount in their host star..
